quote.text: As soon as the land of any country has all become private property, the landlords, like all other men, love to reap where they never sowed.
quote.author: Adam Smith, An Inquiry into the Nature and Causes of the Wealth of Nations, Book I (1776)

macro.title: What It Means For VWRA, Global Bonds, Gold & BTC

stories.kicker: The World · The Gulf War, Day 30
stories.headline: The War Finds Its Second Strait
stories.dek: A blockade declared last weekend hardened this week into gunfire on tankers, as the Gulf war spread from Hormuz to the Bab el-Mandeb — and a ten-day ceasefire offer sat, unanswered, on two desks at once.

stories.headline: Wong Remakes the Cabinet, and Renames a Ministry
stories.dek: A reshuffle unveiled on 22 July promotes first-term MPs straight to full minister, elevates Shanmugam to Senior Minister, and turns Trade and Industry into the Ministry of Energy, Trade and Industry — the clearest sign yet of the "5G" succession taking shape.

stories.headline: The Bill Comes Due — and the Foundry Cashes the Cheque
stories.dek: The first heavyweight second-quarter results landed on 22 July as the real test of whether some $725 billion of 2026 AI spending is turning into revenue. TSMC, which sits between every chip designer and every device, has already been paid.

stories.headline: A 54% Debut Shows the Cooler Market Its Own Face
stories.dek: Lentor Gardens Residences sold just over half its units on opening day — a healthy result that nonetheless fell short of the year's 90%-plus openings, the clearest sign yet that Singapore buyers have turned price-conscious.

stories.headline: Samsung Names Its Glasses, and Almost Nothing Else
stories.dek: At its 22 July Unpacked, Samsung finally detailed its first AI eyewear — a screenless, audio-only device with Gemini at its centre — then declined to give it a price or a firm date. A wider Fold and a much brighter Watch did the actual shipping.

stories.headline: Shelly Asks About Native Thread — and Open Source Beats It There
stories.dek: With its Gen4 hardware already Thread-capable, Shelly is publicly weighing first-party Matter-over-Thread firmware. A third-party open-source project already ships it — a small, clean parable of the vendor-versus-home-lab divide.

stories.headline: Tissot Shrinks Its Most Capable Diver
stories.dek: The Seastar 2000 Professional loses two millimetres and, unusually, some money — a 600-metre, 80-hour automatic that finally fits a normal wrist, and undercuts the watch it replaces.

stories.headline: Comic-Con Opens on a Half-Billion Tailwind
stories.dek: San Diego throws its doors open on 23 July with Marvel's Avengers: Doomsday already breaking records — but the cast is not on stage until Saturday. The floor, for now, is the tease before the reveal.

stories.kicker: The Family Desk · The Digital Home
stories.headline: The Dread Gets a Name — and a Number
stories.dek: A new survey coins "techxiety" and counts the sleep parents lose to it. Useful as a mood reading; thin as evidence — and the honest story underneath is more reassuring than the headline.

stories.headline: An oil bid, a live-again Fed, and a dollar turning up
stories.dek: A widening Gulf war keeps a premium under crude just as a hawkish chair drags July's meeting toward a genuine call on a hike; gold catches a haven bid while bitcoin lags. Market explanation and education, not investment advice — forecasts flagged as forecasts throughout.

stories.headline: Webb Watches a Black Hole Recycle Its Own Dinner
stories.dek: Nearly eight hours of staring into a galaxy 145 million light-years away resolved a faint swirl into an 800-light-year disk feeding a supermassive black hole — and caught the hole reheating and recycling the very gas it feeds on.

stories.headline: Chatfishing: When the Charm Is a Chatbot
stories.dek: Singles are quietly routing their matches' messages through ChatGPT and Claude to write the replies. A modern Turing test for romance — and the apps admit they have no plan to stop it.

stories.headline: "Japanese Walking" — a 2007 Study in a New Outfit
stories.dek: Fast three minutes, slow three minutes, repeat for thirty. The summer's biggest walking trend is legitimate exercise science, well-suited to a mid-40s body. Ignore the "adds seven years" maths.

stories.headline: This Weekend, and the October Rush
stories.dek: A farewell-era K-pop pair lands in town in days; a decade-absent trio returns in October. Book the near one now.

stories.headline: The Middleman's Hour
stories.dek: A foundry banks a record while its customers are punished for spending; a movement taxes the world's oil without pumping a barrel; a chatbot writes the charm between two strangers. Three histories of the party in the middle — a city, a strait and a river of toll-castles — and the one question that has always decided whether we tolerate it.
